info.name: BioStar 2 API
info.description:
Overview

Suprema BioStar 2 API is a JSON-based API. All requests are made to endpoints beginning with https://server_ip|domain_name[:port]/api

The API can be used to perform almost any operation. Following are a few examples of what can be done with Suprema BioStar 2 API:

  • Manage User data
  • Manage Access Group data
  • Manage Doors data
  • Assign an Access Group to one or multiple User
  • Assign a User with one or multiple Access Group
  • Manage Devices
  • And many more...

All requests must be secure (https).

General Information

To call the API, BioStar 2 is mainly using HTTP Methods GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE.

  • GET is used to retrieve data from the server at the specified resource. For example, if you want to retrieve the User data with /users endpoint, then by making a GET request to the endpoint should result with the list of all available users.
  • POST request is used to send data to the server to create or update a resource. The data sent to the server is stored on the body of the HTTP request.
  • PUT is similar to POST in that it creates or updates a resource. The difference between them is that when calling the same PUT requests multiple times it will have the same result. While calling a POST request repeatedly will create the same resource multiple times.
  • DELETE is used to delete resources at the specified endpoint.

There are four parameter location that’s being used on Suprema BioStar 2 API:

  • PATH parameter is located on the URL, usually on the end of an endpoint. For example in /items/{ItemID}, the PATH parameter is ItemID.
  • BODY. An array of string values will be located on the BODY of the HTTP request. The request body needs to be in JSON format.
  • QUERY. Similar to the PATH parameter, QUERY parameter is also located on the URL, the difference is QUERY parameter will be located after a question mark. Example: /items?id=###, the QUERY parameter is id.
  • HEADER. Custom headers that are expected as part of the request.

Path templating refers to the usage of template expressions, delimited by curly braces ({}), to mark a section of a URL path as replaceable using path parameters. Example: https://127.0.0.1:4433/api/some_api/{id}
Above example shows that the required value will be placed on {id}

For AUTHORIZATION details please check POST Login documentation.

These APIs have been added/revised, with changes effective from BioStar 2 v2.9.9. See the Changes section on each API for details.

2025-09-12

⚠️ API URL Trailing Slash No Longer Supported ⚠️

As of version 2.9.9, API endpoints will no longer accept trailing slashes (/) at the end of URLs.

🚫 Invalid:

GET {ip}/api/endpoint/

POST {ip}/api/endpoint/?param=123

✅ Valid:

GET {ip}/api/endpoint

POST {ip}/api/endpoint?param=123

This update affects all endpoint calls with a trailing slash at the end of the path. Please review your integrations and remove any trailing slashes to ensure continued functionality.

This change improves consistency and aligns with stricter URL parsing behavior introduced in recent platform updates.

Affected endpoints in this collection:

List All Access Group v2 POST /api/v2/access_groups/search/

Check Card Availibility GET /api/v2/cards/registered?card_id=123

photo Base64 String User’s profile picture in Base64 format.
photo_exists Boolean Indicates whether the user has a profile picture (true = has a photo, false = no photo).
pin Base64 String User’s encrypted PIN code.
pin_exists Boolean Indicates whether the user has set a PIN (true = PIN set, false = no PIN).
login_id String User’s login identifier.
password_exists Boolean Indicates whether the user has set a password (true = password set, false = no password).
updated_count Number Number of times the user’s data has been updated.
last_modified Number Timestamp of the last data modification.
idx_last_modified Number Internal tracking number for the last modification when personal data is encrypted.
start_datetime ISO Date Date and time when the user’s access period started.
expiry_datetime ISO Date Date and time when the user’s access will expire.
security_level Number User’s security clearance level.
display_duration Boolean Indicates whether the screen display period is enabled.
display_count Boolean Indicates whether screen display count is enabled.
permission Object Defines the user’s operator-level permissions.
inherited Boolean Indicates whether the user inherits properties from a user group (true = inherits, false = does not inherit).
user_group_id Object The user group to which the user belongs.
disabled Boolean Indicates whether the user’s account is disabled (true = disabled, false = active).
expired Boolean Indicates whether the user’s account has expired (true = expired, false = active).
idx_user_id Number Internal tracking number for the user ID in decrypted memory.
idx_user_id_num Number Internal tracking number for user ID when alphanumeric mode is activated in decrypted memory.
idx_name Number Internal tracking number for the user’s name in decrypted memory.
idx_phone Number Internal tracking number for the user’s phone number in decrypted memory.
idx_email Number Internal tracking number for the user’s email address in decrypted memory.
fingerprint_login Boolean Indicates whether the user can log in using a fingerprint.
fingerprint_template_count Number Number of registered fingerprints for the user.
face_count Number Number of registered facial recognition templates for the user.
visual_face_count Number Number of registered visual facial recognition templates.
card_count Number Number of assigned access cards for the user.
qr_count Number Number of assigned QR code credentials.
mobile_count Number Number of assigned mobile access cards.
need_to_update_pw Boolean Indicates whether the user is required to update their password (true = password update required).
version.dbBiostarVersion String Installed version of the BioStar 2 database.
version.biostarVersion String Installed build number of the CGI server.
isMatchVersion Boolean Indicates whether dbBiostarVersion and biostarVersion have the same prefix (true = versions match). Example: 2.9.8.30 and 2.9.8.42 would return true.
notiPopupOpen Boolean The inverse of isMatchVersion. (true = versions do not match).

item.name: Access Group

request.description:
This request is used to make a new Access Group (AG)

BODY Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description
name String Y The name of the AG. Must be unique
description String N AG's description
users Number N List of user(s) id of the AG
user_group Number N List of user group(s) id of the AG
access_levels Number N List of access level(s) id of the AG
floor_levels Number N List of floor level(s) id of the AG

If BioStar 2 does not have license for Elevator module, skip the floor_levels parameter or put it like this: "floor_levels": ""

For numeric value (such as id's), the value can be put on a quote or not.

Response Properties:

Parameter Type Description
AccessGroup Object AccessGroup object
AccessGroup.id Number ID of the Access Group which was created
AccessGroup.name String Name of the Access Group which was created
DeviceResponse Object DeviceResponse object
DeviceResponse.rows Array List of device responses
DeviceResponse.id Number ID of the device which updated it's AG.
DeviceResponse.code Number Response code from the device. 0=Success
DeviceResponse.result Boolean Summary of device response. True when all devices successfully updated (code=0), False when even one is failed (code != 0).

Known errors:

  • Putting "/" at the end of the endpoint

  • name is a required and unique parameter

This API is used to view all available Access Groups.

By default the result will be sorted ascending by id.

response.name: List All Access Group

request.description:
This API is used to see all Access Groups. It provides more concise response rather than the previous (GET /api/access_groups) and parameters to filter the response.

BODY Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description
limit Number Y To limit result by n record(s), 0 to show all
order_by String Y To order result(s) by name or id (false is for ascending and true for descending)

Known errors:

  • Empty body

  • Input other than number on limit parameter

  • Input without key:value pair (name:val or id:val) on order_by parameter

Changes:

  • v2.9.9:

    • Trailing slashes (/) at the end of this endpoint are no longer supported.

request.description:
This endpoint is used to replace Access Group with id {id}/:id values with the one specified by parameters below:

Parameter Type Required Description
name String Y The name of the AG. Must be unique
description String N AG's description
user_group Array N List of user group(s) id of the AG.
To add or delete User Groups, put new ones in this field or remove what you want to delete from the field.
access_levels Array N List of access level(s) id of the AG
floor_levels Array N List of floor level(s) id of the AG
new_users Array N List of user(s) id which will be added to the AG
delete_users Array N List of user(s) id which will be deleted from the AG

Notes:

  • To add/delete multiple, repeat the braces (see users example)

  • When you add/update data, it will remove any previous data and add the one that you input
    Ex: If parameter description have value, and on update this parameter value is left empty, then the previous value will be removed.

  • To skip a record from updating, either skip the parameter or fill the current data on the update field.

  • When updating an AG, parameter user_group need to be filled if existing User Group want to be retained. If this parameter is left empty, the existing User Group(s) in the AG that should be still there will not be there.

Known Errors:

  • Repeating the same value on parameter with multiple options

request.description: This API is used to view BioStar 2 Backup configuration.

This API is used to check if there's any running backup process.

Message response will be True if there's any, and False if there's no running backup process.

This API is used to modify BioStar 2 backup configuration.

BODY Parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
use String Y Toggle true to use Shceduled Backup
cycle String Y Number of backup files to keep (1 to 100)
path String Y Folder path, make sure that the folder exist
minute String Y Minute value for Scheduled Backup (0 to 59)
hour String Y Hour value for Scheduled Backup (0 to 23)
days String Y Date value for Scheduled Backup
month String Y Month value for Scheduled Backup. Leave it as * since currently it only support Daily and Weekly.
weeks String Y Day value for Scheduled Backup. Used when it's configured as Weekly Backup. (mon tue wed thu fri sat sun, default value is ?)
userId String Y UserId of the user who modify the configuration

This API is used to run Backup process.

It is required to have BODY parameters when running this API. Use GET /api/v2/setting/getBackupDbConfig for BODY payload.

It is possible to use a custom wiegand format.

To create a custom format, you can edit the provided formats which is between id number 5 to 14.

BODY parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
id Number N ID of the wiegand format.
name String N Name of the wiegand format.
description String N Description of the wiegand format.
use_facility_code Boolean N Toggle to use facility code.
length Number N Total bits of the card.
id_fields N Encoded input of the ID bits. Facility code will also be placed here.
Use comma as separator.
parity_fields N Encoded input of the parity bits.
parity_types Number N 1=Odd, 2=Even
parity_positions Number N Position of the parity bits. Put it on the same sequence as parity_types.

description.content: ID of the wiegand format which will be updated.

request.description: This API is used to view a smart card layout details.

description.content: ID of the card layout which will be viewed.

request.description:
This API is used to create smart card layout.

Fill the corresponding parameter with the card's specification.

If the card uses default configuration, then simply fill the name and run the API.

This API is used to add a card to BioStar 2.

BODY parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
card_type:id Number Y Type card id of the card. See "View All Card Types" for all type of cards.
0: CSN card
1: Wiegand Card
2: Secure Credential Card (Smart card)
3: Access on Card (Smart card)
4: Mobile CSN Card
5: Wiegand Mobile
6: QR/Barcode
7: BioStar 2 QR
card_type:type Number Y Type allocation of the card type. View the list using /api/cards/types to determine the correct value.
card_id Number Y Input value shown/read when scanning the card.

NOTE:

  • This API will return error if the card_id is already exist on the database. Use "Check Card Availability" API to validate.

  • As per BioStar 2 version 2.9.4, this API will return an error when generating CSN Card with invalid card id.
    Error Code: ACB_ERROR_CODE.415
    Cases of invalid card id:

    • CSN with card id staring with 0

    • CSN with card id using letters

    • More than 32 characters

This API is used to add a card to BioStar 2.

BODY parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
card_type:id Number Y Type card id of the card. See "View All Card Types" for all type of cards.
0: CSN card
1: Wiegand Card
2: Secure Credential Card (Smart card)
3: Access on Card (Smart card)
4: Mobile CSN Card
5: Wiegand Mobile
6: QR/Barcode
7: BioStar 2 QR
card_type:type Number Y Type allocation of the card type. View the list using /api/cards/types to determine the correct value.
card_id Number Y Input value recorded on the card.
display_card_id Number N Input value shown/read when scanning the card.
isUserPhoto Boolean Y Toggle true if the credential will be using the user photo (photo must exist)
isDepartment Boolean Y Toggle true if the credential will be using the user department (department field must be filled)
isTitle Boolean Y Toggle true if the credential will be using the user title (title field must be filled).
start_datetime Date Y Start datetime for the credential availability.
expiry_datetime Date Y End datetime for the credential availability.

NOTE:

  • This API will return error if the card_id is already exist on the database. Use "Check Card Availability" API to validate.

For this example, there's no conversion required since the card id already have 35 bit value.

19044429267 > Decimal
100 0110 1111 0010 0010 1110 1101 1101 0011 > Binary (35 Bits)

Just make sure to use format id which doesn't use facility code.

BODY parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
card_type:id Number Y Type card id of the card. See "View All Card Types" for all type of cards.
0: CSN card
1: Wiegand Card
2: Secure Credential Card (Smart card)
3: Access on Card (Smart card)
4: Mobile CSN Card
5: Wiegand Mobile
6: QR/Barcode
7: BioStar 2 QR
card_type:type Number Y Type allocation of the card type. View the list using /api/cards/types to determine the correct value.
card_id Number Y Input value shown/read when scanning the card.
wiegand_format_id:id Number Y If card_type is wiegand, then fill this parameter:
0: 26 bit format
1: HID 37 bit-H10302
2: HID 37 bit-H10304
3: HID Corporate 1000 (35 bit)
4: HID Corporate 1000 (48 bit)
5~14: Custom formats.

NOTE:

  • This API will return error if the card_id is already exist on the database. Use "Check Card Availability" API to validate.

To input card that is using facility code, there's conversion that needs to be done first.

The process is:

  1. Get the Facility Code and Card ID
  2. Convert it to Binary
  3. Combine the binary value of the Facility Code and Card ID
  4. Convert it to Decimal
  5. Use the converted value on card_id parameter
  6. Don't forget to select the corresponding wiegand_format_id

Conversion when The Card is Using Facility Code

E: Even
O: Odd
C: Facility Code
D: ID

26 bit SIA Standard-H10301
Position: 0-25

Field E CCCCCCCC DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD O
Position 0 1-8 9-24 25

E&O : Not included on the calculation
Bit : 8 bits + 16 bits
Byte: 1 byte + 2 bytes
Hexa: FF + FFFF

Example:
Facility Code + Card ID
20 + 10000 > Decimal
14 + 2710 > Hex
14 2710 > Combined Hex (CH)
1320720 > Converted CH to Decimal. Use this as input

HID 37 bit-H10304
Position: 0-36

Field E CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD O
Position 0 1-16 17-35 36

E&O : Not included on the calculation
Bit : 16 bits + 19 bits

Example:
Facility Code + Card ID
43962 + 123456 > Decimal
1010 1011 1011 1010 + 001 1110 0010 0100 0000 > Binary (16 + 19 bits)
1010 1011 1011 1010 0011 1100 0100 1000 000 > Combined Binary (CB)
23048872512 > Converted CB to Decimal. Use this as input

HID Corporate 1000
Position 0-34

Field O E CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD O
Position 0 1 2-13 14-33 34

E&O : Not included on the calculation
Bit : 12 bits + 20 bits

Example:
Facility Code + Card ID
2543 + 1005255 > Decimal
1001 1110 1111 + 1111 0101 0110 1100 0111 > Binary (12 + 20 bits)
1001 1110 1111 1111 0101 0110 1100 0111 > Combined Binary (CB)
2667534023 > Converted CB to Decimal. Use this as input

HID Corporate 1000 (48bit)
Position 0-47

Field O E CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD O
Position 0 1 2-23 24-46 47

E&O : Not included on the calculation
Bit : 22 bits + 23 bits

Example:
Facility Code + Card ID
2813689 + 4336695
10 1010 1110 1110 1111 1001 + 100 0010 0010 1100 0011 0111 > Binary
1010 1011 1011 1011 1110 0110 0001 0001 0110 0001 1011 1 > Combined Binary (CB)
23602938391607 > Converted CB to Decimal. Use this as input

item.name: User

request.description:
This API returns the next available user ID based on the currently assigned user IDs. If user IDs are numeric, the response will be the next sequential number. If alphanumeric user IDs are enabled, the API will only consider numeric user IDs and return the next available numeric value.

  • If the currently used user IDs are 1, 2, 3, the API response will be 4.

  • If user ID type is configured to alphanumeric, but existing IDs are 1, 2, 3, x4, 4x, the response will still be 4 (since "x4" and "4x" are ignored).

Notes:

  • The API automatically filters out non-numeric user IDs when alphanumeric mode is enabled.

  • This does not create a new user ID, only suggests the next available one.

Response Properties:

Parameter Type Description
User.user_id String The next available user ID.

This API is used to create a new user.

BODY parameters:

Parameters Type Required Description
user_id String Y ID of the User. Unique.

When User ID Type in Setting > Server is set to Number, a number between 1 and 4294967295 can be entered. When Alphanumeric is set, a combination of alphabetic characters and numbers can be entered.
Do not use spaces when entering ID.
Numbers or Alphanumeric characters can be set for user_id. For more details, refer to Setting Section of this documentation.
name String N Name of the user. Up to 48 characters.
user_group_id Number Y ID of the User Group that the user will be under. Refer to User Group Section of this documentation for listing available User Groups.
disabled Boolean N Toggle User active or not; false for active, true for non-active.
start_datetime Date/Time Y Start active period for the User.
The earliest allowable value is 2001-01-01 00:00.
expiry_datetime Date/Time Y End active period for the User.
Value can not exceed 2037-12-31 23:59.
email String N Email address of the User.
department String N Department of the User. Up to 64 characters.
user_title String N Title of the User.
photo Base64 N Photo of the User.
phone String N Phone number of the User.
permission Number N Operator Level of the User.

Use View User by Account Level (GET /api/permissions) to view the list of available Operator Level and its ID).

None: The user has no operator privilege. To use this Operator Level, remove permission parameter from the API Request.
Administrator: The user can use all menus.
User Operator: The user can only use the USER and PREFERENCE menus.
Monitoring Operator: The user can use the MONITORING and PREFERENCE menus and only view the DASHBOARD, USER, DEVICE, DOOR, ZONE and ACCESS CONTROL menus.
Video Operator: The user can only use the VIDEO menu.
T&A Operator: The user can only use the TIME ATTENDANCE menu and only view the USER menu.
User: The user can only view own information and T&A records.
access_groups Number N ID of the Access Group that the User will have access. Refer to Access Group Section of this documentation for listing available Access Groups.
Can enter more than 1 Access Group.
login_id String N Required when permission parameter is not removed.
password String N Required when permission parameter is not removed.
user_ip String N Limit the access of the User so he/she can only login from the registered IP Address.

The user IP can be entered in the format xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx. Each octet can only be entered in numbers between 0 and 255.
Users whose user IP is not registered can log in from any IP.
pin Number N PIN of the user. Maximum 32 digit.

When Setting > Server is set to Number, you can make use of endpoint /api/users/next_user_id to automatically get the next user_id. Refer to GET Find Next Available UserID for the detail of this endpoint.

Response Properties:

Parameter Type Description
total String Total of created user
user_id String Created user's ID
name String Created user's name

Known errors:

  • name is more than 48 characters

  • Using single quote on name

  • Duplicate user_id, email, or login_id

  • Empty user_id, user_group_id, start_datetime, expiry_datetime

  • Using user_group_id:id which doesn't exist

  • Putting "/" at the end of the endpoint

  • Password with 3 or more subsequent letter or number

This API can be used starting from BioStar 2 version 2.9.8.

It is used to create multiple users at once.

Things to be noted when using this API:

  • Parameters which can be used is the same as when creating a user (see Create New User)

  • user_id should be unique.

  • To add a user credential using this API, you must create the credential and include the credential ID in the body.

  • Because syncing users to their devices takes time, it is recommended to add less than 30 users at a time.

This API is used for updating user information in bulk.

This API can be used from BioStar 2 version 2.9.6 onwards.

The difference of this API and /api/users?id=1+2+3 is that this API can update different data of many users at the same time.

Warning: The maximum number of users that can be modified in a single API call is 100.

BODY Parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
user_id String Y User ID of the user. This will be used as the update target.

All user information parameters in /api/users are included.

Response Example:

{
    "UserCollection": {
        "rows": [
            {
                "user_id": "3",
                "ret_code": "0"
            },
            {
                "user_id": "2",
                "ret_code": "224"
            },
            {
                "user_id": "1",
                "ret_code": "0"
            }
        ]
    },
    "Response": {
        "code": "1",
        "link": "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
        "message": "Request is successful, but partially"
    }
}

Response Code:

ret_code presents BioStar 2 response code for modifying each user. The meaning on each code is explained on [BioStar 2 Install Path]\nginx\html\resources\messages_en.

For example ret_code 224, find the number in the properties file: ACB_ERROR_CODE.224 = Cannot use the same previous PW.

"Response.code" could be 0, 1, 8.

  • 0 : Overall success.

  • 1 : Partial success

  • 8 : Overall failed.

HTTP Response Code:

  • 200 OK : Overall success, partial success or overall failed.

  • 500 Internal Server Error: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

item.name: User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)

request.description:
On this example you can see how to use stated parameters to adjust misc. credential values.

Parameters on this example:

Parameter Type Required Description
pin Number N PIN of the User
security_level Number N To set the security level of the credential. The higher the security level is set, the false rejection rate (FRR) gets higher, but the false acceptance rate (FAR) gets lower. Acceptable value: 0 to 5
private_operation_modes
:operation_method Number The first credential of an Authentication Mode (0: ID, 1: Card, 2: Fingerprint/Biometric (Standard), 3: Face)
:operation_mode Number Authentication Mode combination (see below for reference, "-1" = do not use)
:exclude Boolean If Include Device Default Authentication Mode option is on, so when you want to also follow the Auth Modes that the devices have, “private_operation_modes” will have same number of elements that you actually configured. But if Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode is on, you need to add four or more elements because all the values of “operation_method” should be included to override device’s Auth Modes.

Authentication Mode Combination Table:

item.name: User Credential: Misc (Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode)

request.description:
As stated on previous example (User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)), when you want to use Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode then you need to state all values of the Authentication Method.

On this example, the Authentication Mode that's being used is Biometric (operation_method: 2) only (operation_mode: 0) and the other Authentication Method: ID (0), Card (1), and Face (3) are set as do not use (operation_mode: -1) and hidden (exclude: true)

To use Extended Auth Mode simply use code that starts from 11 (refer to Authentication Mode Combination Table on User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)), do note that this mode only supported by FaceStation F2.

This example shows how to assign a card to a User, which is applicable to CSN, Wiegand, Smart, Mobile, and QR/Barcode.

First the card must be enrolled to the database by using POST /api/cards.

After enrolled, use GET /api/cards to view all Cards or GET /api/cards/unassigned to view all Cards which are unassigned.

Select a card from the response and apply it on the body as shown on the example to assign it to the selected user.

This example shows how to update fingerprint data on a User.

The BODY parameters as follows:

Parameter Type Required Description
fingerprint_templates
:finger_mask Boolean N Set to True to mark the fingerprint as a duress fingerprint. When threatened by someone to open the door, the user can authenticate using this fingerprint to send an alarm signal to BioStar 2.
:template0 Raw Y First scan template of the fingerprint
:template1 Raw Y Second scan template of the fingerprint
:isNew Boolean N

Use POST /api/devices/:dev_id/scan_fingerprint to enroll fingerprint
Use POST /api/server_matching/identify_finger to check whether the fingerprint already recorded or not.

  • Raw data for template0 and template1 available after enrolling a fingerprint.

  • Fingerprint(s) used for regular access should not be registered as duress fingerprint.

  • If the fingerprint authentication rate is low, delete the existing fingerprint information and add a new fingerprint.

  • Use an adequate security level. If 1:1 Security Level is too high, the fingerprint authentication rate may be too low or the false rejection rate (FRR) may be too high.

  • For best fingerprint scanning quality, make sure to cover the entire surface of the fingerprint sensor with the finger.

This example shows how to add a Face Credential on a User.

You can add the user's faces if the device supports face authentication.

  • When registering a face, maintain a distance of 40cm to 80cm between the device and the face.
  • Be careful not to change the facial expression. (smiling face, drawn face, wink, etc.)
  • If you do not follow the instructions on the screen, the face registration may take longer or may fail.
  • Be careful not to cover the eyes or eyebrows.
  • Do not wear hats, masks, sunglasses or eyeglasses.
  • Be careful not to display two faces on the screen. Register one person at a time.
  • It is recommended for a user wearing glasses to register both faces with and without glasses.

Parameters on this example:

Parameter Type Required Description
faces Array Y Main container of the credential
:raw_image Raw Y Raw image of the face scan
:templates Array Y Container of the face templates
::template Raw Y Face template of the User

To get data for parameter raw_image and template, use GET /api/devices/:device_id/credentials/face

Use POST /api/v2/devices/search to list all device which can support the selected credentials. See API documentation for details on the usage of the API.

When updating a User, it is required to put the credential data as acquired from GET /api/users/:id
This is done so the data won't be lost after update.

To delete a Face, skip the parameters on using this API.

You can use raw_image value as value on photo parameter of a User.

Visual Face is a credential that captures the user's face with a visual camera. It is different from face information captured with an infrared camera and is only available on devices that support Visual Face.

There are two ways to input a Visual Face: by scanning through a enrollment device, or by uploading an image.

Up to 2 visual faces can be added.
Use GET /api/devices/:device_id/credentials/face to scan Visual Face using an enrollment device.

You can enroll a visual face using FaceStation F2.
Use POST /api/v2/devices/search to list compatible devices.

To upload image as a Visual Face: image file size is up to 10MB and supported file formats are JPG, JPEG and PNG.
Use PUT /api/users/check/upload_picture to check whether the image is suitable for use or not.
BODY example on using Upload Picture:

{
"User": {
 "credentials": {
  "visualFaces": [{
   "template_ex_picture": "/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAAAQABAAD/ Truncated"
  }]
 }}
}

Parameters on this example:

Parameter Type Required Description
visualFaces Array Y Main container of Visual Face credential
:template_ex_normalized_image Base64 Y Cropped image ready for visual face extraction.
:templates Array Y Container of the templates
::credential_bin_type Number Y UNKNOWN = -1
FACE_TEMPLATE = 0
FACE_TEMPLATE_IMAGE = 1
FACE_RAW_IMAGE = 2
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER_2 = 5 (For FaceStation F2)
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER_3 = 9 (For BioStation 3 and W3)
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_NORMALIZED = 7
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_PICTURE = 8
::template_ex Raw Y Visual Face template data which includes FaceStation F2 and the new BioStation 3.
:template_ex_picture Base64 Y Parameter for Upload Picture raw data

Changes:

  • v2.9.6:

    • templateEx changed to template_ex

    • templateExIr removed since it's not used anymore

    • Added credential_bin_type: FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER2 and FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER_3

This API is used to check whether the image that will be uploaded on Visual Face credential is according to specification or not.

Parameter Type Required Description
template_ex_picture Base64 Y Image that will be checked
  • Supported image file size is up to 10MB.
  • Supported image file formats are JPG, JPEG and PNG.

request.description:
This API is used to upload a file to BioStar 2 Server.

Usage of this API is to upload a .csv or .tgz file to BioStar 2 Server Installation path, which after the file can be further processed for importing User Data to the database through POST /api/users/csv_import or POST /api/users/data_import.

Uploaded file will be located on: {{BioStar 2 Installation Path}}\nginx\html\download

Do note that if there's any file with the same name, it will be overwritten.

This API is used to export User Data as .csv file.

This feature is useful when you create users in bulk or when you transfer users to another 3rd party systems.

The location of the exported file is on: {{BioStar 2 Installation Folder}}\nginx\html\download

Result will be sorted by ID.

request.description:
This API is used to import User Data from a .csv file to the BioStar 2 database.

To run this API successfully, it is imperative to run POST /api/attachments first to upload the .csv file to the server install path.

After .csv file got uploaded to the server, put the filename value from the above API to the uri and fileName parameter on this API.

BODY parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
File:uri String Y Path of the .csv file
File:fileName String Y File Name of the .csv file
CsvOption Y Container for available importing options
:start_line Number Y Specify row number to start data import
:import_option Number Y Options when same ID exist in BioStar 2: 1=Preserve Data, 2=Overwrite Data
Query:headers Array Y List of fields on .csv file. Number of fields should match with system. Use GET /api/users/csv_option to see system fields.
Use GET /download/:csv_filename to see the content of the intended .csv file.
Query:columns Array Y List of fields on the BioStar 2. Use GET /api/users/csv_option to get this list.

Fields listed on Query parameter are also used for field mapping between .csv file and BioStar 2 database.

For example field user_id on Query:headers (.csv) will be matched with the first field of Query:columns (BioStar 2). To change this mapping, adjust the sequence on Query:headers as desired (eg. exchange field department with user_id so the value of department will be used on user_id on BioStar 2).

To skip a field from mapping, put empty string ("") on the intended field.

If there's no issue on the import process, the API will shows "Success" response.
If there's issue (duplicate email, etc.), the response will show that the "Request is successful but partially" and will include a filename which contain records that caused the errors which can be downloaded by using GET /download/:csv_filename

If the CSV file to import contains data for custom user fields and the fields do not exist on the server, then the data for the fields will be ignored during the import process.

To enter user information in a language other than English or Korean, save the CSV file in UTF-8 format.

You can store the data file on external storage (USB) and import to BioStar 2 or device. Up to 500,000 users can be moved from server to device or from device to device.

  • The exported data file from devices using older firmware version cannot be imported into BioStar 2. Make sure always use the latest version of firmware.

  • If you use BioStar 2.8.10 or later, data exported from the previous version of BioStar 2 cannot be imported.

  • If you use BioStar 2.8.10 or later, it is impossible to read data from a device that are using older firmware version. Upgrade the device’s firmware to a compatible version. Compatible devices and firmware versions are as follows:

    • BioStation 2 FW 1.9.0 or later > device_type: 8

    • BioStation A2 FW 1.8.0 or later > device_type: 9

    • FaceStation 2 FW 1.4.0 or later > device_type: 10

    • FaceStation F2 FW 1.0.0 or later > device_type: 29

    • X-Station 2 FW 1.0.0 or later > device_type: 32

  • If the fingerprint template format is different, the data file cannot be imported. For example, the data file exported from a device which uses the Suprema fingerprint template format cannot be imported into a device which uses the ISO fingerprint template format.

  • When importing user data with visual faces enrolled from FaceStation F2, the existing data will be retained if there are already visual face data enrolled on BioStar 2 via Upload Image or a mobile device.

BODY Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description
device_type Number Y Device type which the exported data will be applied to
group_id Number Y Group ID of the device. Default value=1
adv_criteria Object Y See Batch Edit with Advanced Search for adv_criteria details.

  • The exported data file includes the profile photo, user ID, name, period, access group, PIN, auth. mode, credentials (face, fingerprint, card, mobile access card, visual face, BioStar 2 QR, QR/Barcode), 1:1 security level.

  • You can export visual face data directly registered from the device only. It is not possible to export the data registered with the other methods, such as image file upload, CSV import, and mobile devices.

  • Be sure that the device is selected correctly. Otherwise, the device cannot recognize the data file.

Response Example:

{
  "File" : {
    "uri" : "BioStar2_20241030_093821.tgz"
  },
  "Response" : {
    "code" : "0",
    "link" : "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
    "message" : "Success"
  }
}

Response Properties:

Parameter Type Description
File Object File object
File.uri String Exported file name
Response Object Response object
Response.code Number Indicates success or failure
Response.link String Suprema link
Response.message String Success or error message

request.description:
This API is used to compare 2 fingerprint scan to determine whether it's the same finger or not.

As shown on the example, it is applied on template0 and template1 where template0 is the 1st scan of the finger and template1 is the 2nd scan.

To get the value for these parameters, use POST /api/devices/:dev_id/scan_fingerprint two times. The first scan for template0 and second scan for template1.

It will return HTTP 200 and shows message result: true on success, HTTP 500 and shows message result: false on failure.

BODY Parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
template0 Base64 Y 1st scan template of the fingerprint
template1 Base64 Y 2nd scan template of the fingerprint
security_level Number N Set the verification security level. Higher number will have stricter requirement.

description.content: ID of the device which used to scan fingerprint.

request.description:
This API is used to verify duplicate fingerprint.

If fingerprint already exist, it will return success (HTTP 200) and response will show the user which currently using the fingerprint.

Use the result of POST /api/devices/:dev_id/scan_fingerprint for the value of parameter template (either the 1st or 2nd scan).

If fingerprint available, it will return server error (HTTP 500) with response showing message "Failed to identify fingerprint.".

BODY Parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
template Base64 Y Template of the fingerprint which will be validated.

This API is used to scan a fingerprint.

On calling this API, it is required to specify the device which will be used as fingerprint scanner. It is done by specifying Device ID on the API Path, which marked by :dev_id.

The procedure to add a fingerprint as follows:
- Scan a finger using this API
- Use POST /api/server_matching/identify_finger to check if it's already exist
- If it doesn't exist, then run a second scan using this API for the same finger as the first scan
- Compare 1st and 2nd scan result using POST /api/devices/:dev_id/verify_fingerprint to make sure that 1st and 2nd scan are the same finger
- Assign the enrolled finger to a user using POST|PUT /api/users/:id

BODY parameters on this API as follows:

Parameter Type Required Description
enroll_quality Number N Set a fingerprint enrollment quality level. Any fingerprint which does not meet the quality requirement will not be enrolled.
raw_image Boolean N Set this option to view the original image when a fingerprint is scanned.

description.content: ID of the device which will be used to scan fingerprint.

request.description:
This API is used to update Device's homescreen.

Before running this API, you must set the Device's homescreen parameter to either Notice or Logo using Update Device API (/api/devices/:id)

{
  "Device": {
    "display": {
      "background": "0"
      "background_theme": "2"
    }
  }
}
Parameter Description
background 0 = Default
2 = Slide Show
background_theme 0 = Normal
1 = Notice
2 = Logo

To set it as Slide Show mode, set both background and background_theme value to 2.

Then run this API to adjust the display accordingly.

BODY Parameter on this API:
Parameter Type Required Description
DeviceCollection.total Number Y Total number of devices which will be updated.
DeviceCollection.rows.id String Y ID of the device which will be updated. May contain multiple value by repeating the parameter separated by comma (,). Do note that total number of device need to be the same as total number of device listed.
ResourceDataCollection.total Number Y Total number of data which will be applied.
ResourceDataCollection.type String Y 1 = Use this if background_theme is set to Notice.
2 = Use this if background_theme is set to Logo.
3 = Use this to set it as Logo Slide Show.
ResourceDataCollection.rows.slot Number Y Data display sequence. For Type 1 and 2 set it as 0.
ResourceDataCollection.rows.data Base64 Y Data which will be used for homescreen display. Use base64 encoding for its value.

item.name: Delete All Event Logs in Device

request.description:
Delete all event logs stored inside one or more devices.

This endpoint instructs selected devices to remove all event logs stored locally on the device.

Deleted logs cannot be recovered and cannot be restored from the server.

Before using this API, ensure that the device event logs have already been successfully uploaded to the server.

Devices that do not store logs are automatically excluded from the deletion process:

  • Slave devices

  • Virtual devices

  • Wiegand devices

Permissions

This endpoint requires Administrator privileges.

Behavior

Case 1: Clear logs for specific devices

If id contains one or more device IDs, the endpoint clears logs only for those devices.

Example

DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=1001 1002 1003
OR
DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=1001+1002+1003

Case 2: Clear logs for all devices

If id=*, the endpoint targets all devices.

Example

DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=*

Case 3: Clear logs for filtered devices

If id=*, optional filters may be used to restrict the target set.

Example

DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=*&group_id=10&offset=0&limit=50

This means:

  • target all devices

  • restrict to group 10

  • apply paging with offset=0 and limit=50

Notes

  • This API deletes only the event logs stored in the device.

  • Event logs already stored on the server are not affected.

  • Deleted logs cannot be recovered.

  • Devices that do not store logs (Slave, Virtual, Wiegand) are automatically excluded.

request.description:
This API is used to view all available door groups.

It is required to put {} on the body parameter to run the API successfully.

This API is used to view all door groups while sorting the response using available fields.

Parameter Type Required Description
order_by String Y Sort the response records by value which inputted on this parameter
:boolean String Y Value which the sorting will be based on. Available fields: id, name, depth

This API is used to create door groups.

BODY parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
parent_id:id Number Y Parent ID of the group.
depth Number Y Depth of the group in accordance with the parent group (parent depth+1). Value between 1 to 8.
name String Y Name of the group. Do not put empty value on this parameter. Max 48 characters.

Known issue:
- Duplicate group name within one parent group
- depth value not within 1-8
- Using parent_id which doesn't exist will result in success but the created group will not be visible.
- Using empty value on name will success creating a door with name: undefined. This door can't be deleted. To resolve this, update the door name then it will be able to be deleted.

This API is used to delete door group.

Put the door's ID on the id parameters of this API to delete door.

Note:
- Deleting parent group will also delete child group
- Deleting groups with door(s) will also remove door(s) under that group. This will consequently remove doors from Access Levels
- Can't delete groups with parent_id which doesn't exist
- Can't delete groups with undefined name

description.content: ID of the door which will be deleted

request.description:
This API is used to view door records from BioStar 2 database.

BODY parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
limit Number N Limit the number of records on the response according to the value specified
search_text String N Find records with the specified string
door_group_id Number N Limit the records to only those with door_group_id specified
order_by String N Sort the response by stated value (id, door_group_id.name). Toggle boolean to switch ascending or descending.

It is required to at least put {} on the body for the API to run correctly.

This API is used to create a door.

BODY parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
name String Y Name of the door
description String N Description of the door
door_group_id:id Number Y ID of the Door Group which the door will be under
open_timeout Number N Configure the maximum allowed time for the door to remain open. Default: 10.
open_duration Number N Set the duration for which the door will remain open after a user authentication is completed. When the authentication is successful, the relay will be activated for the set time. When this time elapses, the relay no longer sends the signal to the door. Open Time may vary depending on the type of door lock used.
open_once Boolean N Lock when door is closed: When the door sensor detects that the door is closed, the door is locked. Set this option to false if unconditional_lock is true. Set true to enable this parameter. Default: false.
unconditional _lock Boolean N Use Automatic Door: When using an automatic door as an entrance door, a relay can operate regardless of the status of a door sensor. Set this option as false if open_once is set to true.
relay_output_id:device_id:id Number Y Device ID which the relay will be used to control the door lock.
relay_output_id:relay_index Number Y Relay index of the device which will be used.
exit_button_input_id:device_id:id Number Y Device ID which will be used as exit control.
exit_button_input_id:input_index Number Y Exit index of the device which will be used.
exit_button_input_id:type Boolean Y 1: Normally Closed, 0: Normally Open
sensor_input_id:device_id:id Number Y Device ID which will be used as sensor control.
sensor_input_id:input_index Number Y Sensor index of the device which will be used.
sensor_input_id:type Boolean Y 1: Normally Closed, 0: Normally Open
sensor_input_id:apb_use_door_sensor Boolean Y Set whether to use the door sensor when using Entry Confirmed APB.

This API is used to update a Door details.

Leave the values to be edited, and remove everything else.

This API is used to view various door status information such as the door status, relay status, alarm and last event.

  • Fire alarm unlocked: A state where the door designated as a fire alarm zone is unlocked because a fire has broken out.
  • Manual Lock: A state where the door is locked because the administrator has locked it manually.
  • Manual Unlock: A state where the door is unlocked and able to enter without an authentication because the administrator has unlocked it manually.
  • Schedule Locked: A state where the door is locked by the schedule that has been set.
  • Schedule Unlocked: A state where the door is unlocked by the schedule that has been set.
  • Normal: A state where a user can enter the door after an authentication.

This API is used to create elevator.

The minimum values is name, elevator_group_id(id, name), reader_devices(id, name), control_device_id(id, name, device_type_id), floors(name, order, elevator_device_id, relay_index). The rest can be removed.

Controller - control_device_id
Select a device that controls the elevator access permission.
▪ Only a master device can be selected.
▪ You can select it from the list of registered devices.

Reader - reader_devices
Select a device you intend to use for authentication.
▪ You can select a device among the master device, slave device, and Wiegand device.
▪ You can select up to 4 readers.
▪ OM-120 cannot be set as the reader.

Module - module_devices
Select OM-120 to control the elevator button relay.
▪ Only OM-120 can be selected.

Total Number of Floors - floors
Enter the total number of floors that you can move using the elevator.
▪ Up to 192 floors can be entered.

request.description:
This API is used to save/update/delete Quick Action. It is accessible in BioStar 2 version 2.9.5 and later.

To CREATE: Fill in all Quick Action(s) parameter (excluding ID). It is possible to create multiple Quick Action in one payload.
In the case creating new one while there's existing Quick Action(s), the new one doesn't require ID while the existing require it.

To DELETE: Fill in all Quick Action(s) parameter (including ID). Input all Quick Action(s) besides the one that will be deleted. To delete all, simply send an empty payload.

To UPDATE: Fill in all Quick Action(s) parameter (including ID). Input all Quick Action(s) including any which will not be updated.

BODY Parameter:

Parameter Type Required Description
name String Y Quick Action name.
description String Y Quick Action description.
confirm_before_run String Y Confirmation before run (enabled/disabled). Y = enabled, N = disabled.
operator_level_ids Array Y List Operator Level ID(s) which allowed to run the Quick Action.
trigger_action_ids Array Y List Trigger & Action ID(s) which will be executed when Quick Action triggered.
order_number Number Y Quick Action order number.
id Number N Quick Action's ID. Will be generated after Quick Action created. Required to be filled when update or adding.

Response Example:

{
  "Response": {
    "code": "0"
  }
}

Response Properties:

- Response : The web server response.
    - code : success/error code.

This API uses the following error codes:

  • 400 Bad Request: The request was malformed or missing required parameters.

  • 401 Unauthorized: The API key provided was invalid or missing.

  • 404 Not Found: The requested resource was not found.

  • 500 Internal Server Error: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

This API is used to test BioStar 2 connection with Windows Active Directory.

Successful connection will return code : 0.

BODY Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description
serverAddress String Y IP Address of the Domain Controller
userName String Y User Name which will be used to connect to the Domain Controller
password String Y User Name's corresponding password
userDomainName String Y BaseDN of the Domain Controller
useSSL Boolean Y Option to use encryption when communicating with a Windows Active Directory server.

Install Active Directory Certificate Services and set the keystore password by referring to Active Directory Encryption.
adlogin Boolean Y Enable logging into BioStar 2 using the Windows Active Directory account.
RedirectionUri String Y Required for EntraId interworking.
adEnable String Y If BioStar2 is upgraded from a version earlier than 2.9.9, it needs to be set to 'U'. Otherwise, set to 0.
adType String Y Interworking target classification.
Active Directory: A
EntraId: E
prevAdType String Y It is required to enter the previous connection information when modifying the interworking target. If there is no change, enter the current connection information.
prevDomainName String Y It is required to enter the previous connection information when modifying the interworking target. If there is no change, enter the current connection information.
prevServerAddr String Y It is required to enter the previous connection information when modifying the interworking target. If there is no change, enter the current connection information.
resetSync Boolean Y Delete existing users and user groups due to changes in connection information and set them to true if new settings are required. default is false.
scheduleSyncInterval Number Y Set the time interval for automatic synchronization.
0: Manual synchronization
30 to 10080: Automatic synchronization
syncEvtCheck Boolean Y Settings for whether to leave event logs on BioStar2.
true: event logs are kept on BioStar 2 as usual.
syncType String Y Toggle automatic synchronization.
Manual synchronization: N
Automatic synchronization: A

See these articles for more information on connecting BioStar 2 with Active Directory:

  • https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/solutions/articles/24000044140

  • https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/solutions/articles/24000041940--biostar-2-how-to-confirm-active-directory-information/en

Changes:

  • v2.9.9:

    • Supports both Active Directory and EntraID

    • Added parameter: RedirectionUri, adEnable, adType, prevAdType, prevDomainName, prevServerAddr, resetSync, scheduleSyncInterval, syncEvtCheck, syncType.

To run BioStar 2 AD APIs, it is required to be connected to the AD server.

This API is used to get User Group information from the Domain Controller.

It is required to put domain name information on the body.

The response value is returned in JSON format. At this time, Collection.rows.userGroups is used for the next user group update.

Based on the user group information queried in User Group Search, the user group which will be linked is updated.

Update the user group and save it to the Biostar server.

BODY Parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
allUserGroup Boolean Y Toggle True when selecting all User Groups.
dn String Y Domain Name
lastSyncVersion Number Y Set to 0
userGroups Array Y Put Collection.rows.userGroups from User Group Search
userGroupFilters Array Y Place the id and name of the user groups to be synchronized into an array.

This API is used to set the user field configuration.

The payload is in the form of a JSON array.
Each array has BioStar 2 column name (columnm), AD column name (adcolumnm), whether to use (use), and column description (columnmdescription).

[

{"columnm":"usrid","adcolumnm":"sAMAccountName","use":"true","columnmdescription":"userid"} {"columnm":"nm","adcolumnm":"displayName" ,"use":"true","columnmdescription":"User NAME"}

]

If you set the payload as above, you can sync the username as well.

This API is used to synchronizes AD user information with Biostar 2.
If there is no user group change or field reorganization then only this synchronization operation is sufficient.

serverAddress, userName, password, userDomainName, and useSSL must be sent in the payload.

lastSyncTime, lastSyncResult, lastUpdateTime fields on response are "recent sync time with ad server", "recent sync result", "recent update time of ad server information",

This API is used to set the SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) for sending emails.

BODY parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
name String Y Enter the SMTP server name.
smtp_server_addr String Y Enter the SMTP server address. SMTP server address is the same form as 'smtp. Email Service Provider.com', and you can check it on the settings screen of email to use as an SMTP.
smtp_server_port String Y Enter the port number of the SMTP server. you can check it on the settings screen of email to use as an SMTP.
smtp_username String Y Enter the account of the SMTP service.
smtp_password String Y Enter the password of the SMTP service.
smtp_security String Y Select security type.
smtp_sender String Y Enter the email address of the sender.
description String N Enter the description.

Note:

  • When using the SMTP server as an email account with two-factor authentication and change the password of the account, note the following: Once you set up two-factor authentication, the SMTP password is the same as the app password generated using two-factor authentication, not the password of the email account. At this time, if the password of the email account is changed, the app
    password is automatically deleted, and the SMTP password is no longer available. When changing the password for the email account, regenerate the app password and then set the SMTP password again.

Max. Simultaneous Server Matching Count depends on the PC's CPU performance.

14: ToM_AoC
15: ToM_SCC
  • When creating mobile access cards, Airfob portal sites are divided into regular and dynamic
  • The startDate and endDate fields are used when the sites are dynamic sites

ToM

  • ToM uses Extend mode for personal authentication mode when creating a card
  • If another device supports ToM in the future, a value mapped to cardAuthMode (standard) and cardAuthModeEx (extend) should be added for compatibility with devices that do not support Extend
  • In API, if you put in the cardAuthModeEx value, the server internally maps the cardAuthMode value

Card AuthMode Mapping Table:

22 - Card + Face 3 - Card + Fingerprint

request.description:
This API is used to view events registered on BioStar 2.

BODY parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
limit Number Y Limit the response records by the value stated on this parameter
conditions Array N When in use, certain key-value is mandatory.
conditions:column String Y Field which the conditions will be based from
conditions:operator Number Y Condition Operator
EQUAL : 0,
NOT_EQUAL : 1,
CONTAINS : 2,
BETWEEN : 3,
LIKE : 4,
GREATER : 5,
LESS : 6

For field encrypted in encryption mode (ex. user_id.user_id), it only supports Equal (0), Not Equal (1), and Contains(2).
conditions:values Datetime Y Value of the conditions
orders Array N When in use, certain key-value is mandatory.
orders:column String Y Field which the sorting will be based on
orders:descending Boolean N Toggle true to set the sorting as descending

When query events through Search Events (/api/events/search), events that contain image logs include the following fields in the response:

"image_id": {
  "image_data": "1755062341_547838635_159337",
  "image_type": "JPG",
  "photo": "false"
}

The image_data value can be used to retrieve the corresponding image file.

request.description:
You can manage and control the population density of certain areas with Occupancy Limit Zone to prevent overcrowding. You can also monitor the occupancy limit zone status or set an alert to get notified when the zone has reached capacity.

Up to 100 occupancy limit zones can be added.

BODY parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
is_enabled Deactivate the occupancy limit zone. If the zone is deactivated, both Count that refers to the number of users in the zone and Count Bypass settings will be initialized. Select Active to enable it.
name String Enter an occupancy limit zone name. Name can be up to 48 characters in length and cannot be set to the same name as other zones.
zone_type Number View the zone type.
status Number
limit_count Number Enter the number of users who will be restricted from entering the zone. If the number of users in the zone reaches the limit, entry will be restricted. You can enter a number from 0 to 10,000, and if it is set to 0, people can enter the zone without limit.
entry_devices Number Select a device to use for entry. You can select a device from the list of added devices.
exit_devices Number Select a device to use at exit. You can select a device from the list of added devices.
almost_full_alert_setting:level Number Set an alert to an administrator or set to save event logs before Count reaches the occupancy limit.
almost_full_alert_setting:step_1 Number The first alert for occupancy limit.
almost_full_alert_setting:step_2 Number The second alert for occupancy limit.
auto_reset_count_time:is_enabled Boolean Toggle to set the time to automatically reset occupancy limit Count.
auto_reset_count_time:minute Number The Count will be initialized at the time you set every day.
network_fail_action Number Set whether to allow users to enter or leave when a network error occurs in the device set in the zone. If you set it to Allow Entry and Exit, it stops entry restrictions when the device loses network connection, and people can enter the zone even if Count in the zone exceeds the occupancy limit.
bypass_groups:acsgruid Number Set Count Bypass by selecting bypass groups to allow users in the group to enter the zone at any time without affecting the number of Count. The Bypass Count column displays the number of people from Count Bypass in the zone list.
It is not able to use the Count Bypass feature when you set Thermal & Mask Check Mode as Check without authentication while using the device with a thermal camera.
Up to 16 bypass groups can be added.

Notes:

  • Devices and firmware versions that are compatible with the occupancy limit zone features are as follows:
    • FaceStation F2 firmware version 1.1.0 or later
    • FaceStation 2 firmware version 1.5.0 or later
  • A device cannot be assigned to the entry device and exit device simultaneously.
  • You can set entry devices and exit devices up to 128 in total.
  • A device using Dual Authentication cannot be set as the entry or exit device.
  • Set the time of Auto Count Reset based on UTC in the country where the zone is located. For example, if the time is set to 00:00 in UTC+9, the count is automatically reset at 01:00 in UTC+10.
  • You can set up two alerts for Count Alert and only enter numbers smaller than the Limit value. The values of both alerts must be different.

Anti-passback zone provides an enhanced function than the door based anti-passback feature.

Parameter Type Required Description
name String Y Enter an anti-passback name.
description String N Enter an anti-passback description.
zone_type Number Y View the zone type.
is_global Boolean Y It is possible to set either Local or Global for the range of zone application. If Local is set, the zone can be set only with the entry devices and devices connected with RS-485, and if Global is set, the zone can be set with all devices enrolled in BioStar 2.
enable Boolean Y Toggle to disable the anti-passback zone.
status N
apb_type Boolean Y Select an Anti-passback type.
reset_time Number Y You can set a time period so that all anti-passback violations can be deleted. This allows the user to be granted access after the time period. The maximum possible duration is 7 days (10080 minutes). If set to 0, anti-passpack violations will not be deleted and the users who have previously violated the anti-passback rule will not be granted access.
apb_use_door_sensor Boolean Y 0=OFF, 1=ON, 2=Door Config.
You can set the range to apply the anti-passback. If Entry Confirmed APB is set to ON, the anti-passback is applied according to the actual operation of the door that the entry and exit device are configured. If this option is set to OFF, the rule is applied according to the user's authentication regardless of the door operation. When set to Follows door configuration, the anti-passback rule is applied according to the setting of the Use sensor when Entry Confirmed APB enabled option of the door.
entry_devices:id Number Y Select a device to use for entry.
exit_devices:id Number Y Select a device to use at exit.
bypass_groups:id Number N Select an access level. Users who have the access level will not be restricted by the anti-passback rule.
alarm_actions Array N Choose the operation to be triggered when an APB violation occurs.
apb_fail_action Number N 0=Open by Auth ,1=Open by Auth & Record APB Log ,2=Door Locked & Record APB Log.
It is possible to set the door operation in case the communication between BioStar 2 and the device where anti-passback is set has been lost. Setting is available when Global is set for Mode. When Open by auth is set, the door opens when the user has been authenticated normally. When Open by auth & record APB log is set, an anti-passback violation alarm occurs and the door opens. When Door locked & record APB log is set, an anti-passback violation alarm occurs and the door does not open.

This endpoint allows administrators to reset the Anti-Passback state within a zone. It can be applied either to all users in the zone or to a specific set of users.

Body Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description
ZoneCollection.rows.id String Y The ID of the APB zone to be cleared.
UserType Integer Y Specifies the clear mode:
1 = Clear APB for all users.
2 = Clear APB for specific users.
User Array N List of user objects with user_id. Required when UserType = 2.

Response Codes:

  • 200 OK: Request successful

  • 400 Bad Request (Code 671): Zone ID mismatch (zone not found)

  • 401 Unauthorized: Login required or invalid session token

The muster zone is used as a place where users gather when an emergency occurs. It can also be used for the purpose of monitoring the number of users and list of users in a specific area, or for notifying the manager of alarms and alerts when a user stays in a specific area for a long time.

BODY parameter on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
name String Y Enter a muster zone name.
zone_type Number Y View the zone type.

enable Boolean N Toggle to disable the muster zone.
status Number N

entry_devices:id Number Y Select a device to use for entry.
exit_devices:id Number Y Select a device to use at exit.
time_limit Number Y Set the maximum amount of time that user can stay in the zone. It can be set up to 4320 minutes, and an alarm occurs when the user stays in the muster zone exceeding the specified time.
alarm_actions Array N Set the alarm action to carry out when a specific event occurs at the muster zone.

When intrusion alarm zone is used, you can detect trespassing of an unauthorized user to a designated zone without permission.

arm_inputs:device:id Number Y You can set the arming and disarming by device or input signal.

Interlock zone monitors the status of two or more doors by door sensor and relay state to control that one door cannot be opened or close if other doors are open or
unlocked. You can also disable access if a user stays within the zone.

  • An interlock zone can be configured with up to 4 doors.
  • An interlock zone can only set the doors with the devices connected to the CoreStation.
  • A device set as an interlock zone cannot be set to another zone.
  • A door set as an interlock zone cannot be set to another zone other than the fire alarm zone.

BODY parameters on this API:

name String Y Enter an interlock zone name.

enable Number Y You can disable the interlock zone. input 1 to enable it.
inter_lock:doors:id Number Y Select the doors to include in the interlock zone. You must select at least two doors that are the door sensor is connected.
sensor_inputs:device:id Number Y If a user stays in the zone, this option can prevent others from entering the zone. Input the device ID which will be used as sensor.
sensor_inputs:input_index Number Y Index of the sensor which will be used.
sensor_inputs:duration Number Y Set the duration.
sensor_inputs:normal_open Number Y Set status between Normally Open or Closed.
sensor_inputs:input_type String Y Type of the input.
alarm_actions Array N Set the alarm action to carry out when a specific event occurs at the interlock zone.

There will be a key called bs-session-id, which is a consecutive strings of hex digits located on the header of the response:
bs-session-id: 643f64efb7114485a2fd89de17ca13fb

This value will be used as authorization of the APIs that will be called. To apply this value, put it on the header of the API that will be called with key name bs-session-id.

After logging in, you can use this API to view event log(s).

BODY parameters on this API:

Parameter Type Required Description
limit Number N Limit the response records by the value stated on this parameter
conditions:column String Field which the conditions will be based from
conditions:operator Number Condition Operator
EQUAL : 0,
NOT_EQUAL : 1,
CONTAINS : 2,
BETWEEN : 3,
LIKE : 4,
GREATER : 5,
LESS : 6
conditions:values Datetime Value of the conditions
orders:column String Field which the sorting will be based on
orders:descending Boolean Toggle true to set the sorting as descending

There are two main components on this API: conditions and orders.

On conditions, you can put multiple criteria to filter the API response according to your need.

While on orders, it is used to sort the response according to specified parameter, either ascending or descending.

On this example, there are three criteria on the conditions component:

  • First criteria filter the response according to datetime parameter (column: datetime) with date range between (operator: 3) value specified on values: [].
  • Second criteria filter the response to show event log where the device id (column: device_id) is not (operator: 1) as specified (values []).
  • Third criteria filter the response to show event logs which have the specified value (values []) as its id (column:id, operator: 0).
  • The relation between multiple criteria on a single API call is AND.

For the orders component on this example, it will sort the response according to parameter datetime (column: datetime) ascending (descending: false).

Do note that the datetime that is shown on the response is based on UTC-0, so if you want to view event log from a device which use UTC+8, then you need to convert the datetime from UTC+8 to UTC-0 and use the conversion result as criteria value on the conditions.

Example:

  • Device: UTC+8. Event log occurred when datetime on the device is 2022-06-02 01:37
  • Convert the datetime to UTC-0 : 2022-06-01 17:37
  • Use this converted value as criteria value on the condition

This example shows how to view event log from device with id: 543308153 (device timezone set as UTC+8) and log with datetime between 2022-06-01 00:05 to 2022-06-30 23:58.

Note that the datetime used on the API criteria is different from what the user would like to see. This is because the device was set to use UTC+8, so to view the correct log, use datetime which have been converted to UTC-0.

request.description:
Use this API to scan a fingerprint.

On calling this API, it is required to specify the device which will be used as fingerprint scanner. It is done by specifying Device ID on the API Path, which marked by :dev_id.

The procedure to add a fingerprint as follows:
- Scan a finger using this API
- Use POST /api/server_matching/identify_finger to check if it's already exist
- If it's not exist, then run a second scan using this API for the same finger
- Compare 1st and 2nd scan result using POST /api/devices/:dev_id/verify_fingerprint to make sure that 1st and 2nd scan are the same finger
- After running Verify Fingerprint Scan, keep the value of template 0 and 1 to assign the enrolled finger to a user using POST|PUT /api/users/:id

BODY parameters on this API as follows:

Parameter Type Required Description
ScanFingerprintOption
:enroll_quality Number Y Set a fingerprint enrollment quality level. Any fingerprint which does not meet the quality requirement will not be enrolled.
:raw_image Boolean Y Set this option to view the original image when a fingerprint is scanned.
noblockui Boolean Y

After preparing available user_id and credential(s), use this API to create a new user using the basic configuration.

For applying configuration beyond this, such as credential(s) or custom fields, refer to the subsequent example.

The BODY parameters are:

Parameters Type Required Description
user_id String Y ID of the User. Unique.

When User ID Type in Setting > Server is set to Number, a number between 1 and 4294967295 can be entered. When Alphanumeric is set, a combination of alphabetic characters and numbers can be entered.
Do not use spaces when entering ID.
Numbers or Alphanumeric characters can be set for user_id. For more details, refer to Setting Section of this documentation.
name String N Name of the user. Up to 48 characters.
user_group_id Number Y ID of the User Group that the user will be under. Refer to User Group Section of this documentation for listing available User Groups.
disabled Boolean N Toggle User active or not; false for active, true for non-active.
start_datetime Date/Time Y Start active period for the User.
expiry_datetime Date/Time Y End active period for the User.
email String N Email address of the User.
department String N Department of the User. Up to 64 characters.
title String N Title of the User.
photo Base64 N Photo of the User.
phone String N Phone number of the User.
permission Number N Operator Level of the User.

None: The user has no operator privilege. To use this Operator Level, remove permission parameter from the API Request.
Administrator: The user can use all menus.
User Operator: The user can only use the USER and PREFERENCE menus.
Monitoring Operator: The user can use the MONITORING and PREFERENCE menus and only view the DASHBOARD, USER, DEVICE, DOOR, ZONE and ACCESS CONTROL menus.
Video Operator: The user can only use the VIDEO menu.
T&A Operator: The user can only use the TIME ATTENDANCE menu and only view the USER menu.
User: The user can only view own information and T&A records.
access_groups Number N ID of the Access Group that the User will have access. Refer to Access Group Section of this documentation for listing available Access Groups.
Can enter more than 1 Access Group.
login_id String N Required when permission parameter is not removed.
password String N Required when permission parameter is not removed.
user_ip String N Limit the access of the User so he/she can only login from the registered IP Address.

The user IP can be entered in the format xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx. Each octet can only be entered in numbers between 0 and 255.
Users whose user IP is not registered can log in from any IP.
pin Number N PIN of the user. Maximum 32 digit.

When Setting > Server is set to Number, you can make use of endpoint /api/users/next_user_id to automatically get the next user_id. Refer to GET Find Next Available UserID for the detail of this endpoint.

Known errors:

  • name is more than 48 characters
  • Using single quote on name
  • Duplicate user_id, email, or login_id
  • Empty user_id, user_group_id, start_datetime, expiry_datetime
  • Using user_group_id:id which doesn't exist
  • Putting "/" at the end of the endpoint
  • Password with 3 or more subsequent letter or number
  • Start and expiry date difference is more than 30 years

Use GET /api/setting/custom_fields to view available Custom Fields.

There are 3 type of Custom Fields: Text Input, Number Input and Combo Box. Text Input and Combo Box will accept any value, and Number Input will only receive number.

Body parameter for Creating/Updating Custom Field on a User:

Parameter Type Required Description
item String N Content of the Custom Field
custom_field:id Number Y ID of the Custom Field

To skip create/update a Custom Field, skip it from the Body.

On this example you can see how to use stated parameters to adjust misc. credential values.

private_operation_modes
:operation_method Number The first credential of an Authentication Mode (0: ID, 1: Card, 2: Fingerprint/Biometric (Standard), 3: Face)
